title: release podman binary packages
body:
### Feature request description

The operating system's podman package can not catch the podman's release cycle. If a user want to use latest podman features, he needs to build podman from scratch.

Is it possible to publish podman binary packages in GitHub? nerdctl is a good example.

Please include the latest tools like crun, netavark in the binary package.

### Suggest potential solution

Publish podman binary packages along with podman release.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
The project relies on fedora for packaging, and on coreos for the podman machine.

* https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15682

As @afbjorklund points out we have no plans to support this.

If you want the latest there are already repos available, i.e. podman-next https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/
Or the kubic repo for debian/ubuntu as mentioned here: https://podman.io/docs/installation#debian

It might be worth to mention it in the release notes, that it is for source code only ?

"Please wait a few hours until it is in Fedora, and a few weeks until it is in CoreOS"
